to.p: cause to be inspected from time to time any—

ul.li: (a) private children’s home in England;
(b) premises in which a child who is being looked after by a local authority is living;
(c) premises in which a child who is being accommodated by or on behalf of a local authority in the exercise of education functions or a voluntary organisation is living;
(d) premises in which a child who is being accommodated by or on behalf of a Local Health Board,
Special Health Authority ... ,
National Health Service trust or NHS foundation trust or pursuant to arrangements made by the Appropriate National Authority,
NHS England or an integrated care board under the National Health Service Act 2006 is living;
(g) premises in which a privately fostered child,
or child who is treated as a foster child by virtue of paragraph 9 of Schedule 8,
is living or in which it is proposed that he will live;
(h) premises on which any person is acting as a child minder;
(j) care home in England or independent hospital used to accommodate children;
(k) premises which are provided by a local authority and in which any service is provided by that authority under Part III;
(l) school or college providing accommodation for any child;
(a) the Appropriate National Authority is the Welsh Ministers;
and (b) the inspection relates to a private children’s home or a care home (see,
instead the Regulation and Inspection of Social Care (Wales) Act 2016 (anaw 2)).

to.p: be conducted by a person authorised to do so by the Appropriate National Authority.

for.p: an officer of a local authority

to.p: be authorised except with the consent of that authority.
require any person of a kind mentioned in subsection (5) to furnish it with such information, or allow it to inspect such records (in whatever form they are held), relating to—

ul.li: (a) any premises to which subsection (1) or,
in relation to Scotland,
subsection (1)(h) or (i) applies;
(b) any child who is living in any such premises;
(c) the discharge by the Appropriate National Authority of any of its functions under this Act;
or (d) the discharge by any local authority of any of their functions under this Act,
as the Appropriate National Authority may at any time direct
(a) the Appropriate National Authority is the Welsh Ministers;
and (b) the inspection relates to a private children’s home or a care home (see,
instead the Regulation and Inspection of Social Care (Wales) Act 2016 (anaw 2)).

for.p: any person inspecting any home or other premises under this section

ul.li: (a) inspect the children there;
and (b) make such examination into the state and management of the home or premises and the treatment of the children there as he thinks fit
(a) the Appropriate National Authority is the Welsh Ministers;
and (b) the inspection relates to a private children’s home or a care home (see,
instead the Regulation and Inspection of Social Care (Wales) Act 2016 (anaw 2)).

for.p: any person authorised by the Appropriate National Authority to exercise the power to inspect records conferred by subsection (4)

to.p: be entitled at any reasonable time to have access to, and inspect and check the operation of, any computer and any associated apparatus or material which is or has been in use in connection with the records in question.

for.p: any person authorised by the Appropriate National Authority to exercise the power to inspect records conferred by subsection (4)

to.p: require— (i) the person by whom or on whose behalf the computer is or has been so used; or (ii) any person having charge of, or otherwise concerned with the operation of, the computer, apparatus or material, to afford him such reasonable assistance as he may require.

for.p: a person authorised to inspect any premises under this section

to.p: have a right to enter the premises for that purpose, and for any purpose specified in subsection (4), at any reasonable time.
produce some duly authenticated document showing his authority to do so

condition.p: so required.

to.p: intentionally obstruct another in the exercise of that power.
provide by order for subsections (1), (4) and (6) not to apply in relation to such homes, or other premises, as may be specified in the order.
without prejudice to section 104, make different provision with respect to each of those subsections.
(with the consent of the Treasury) defray or contribute towards

ul.li: (a)any fees or expenses incurred by any person undergoing approved child care training (b)any fees charged,
or expenses incurred,
by any person providing approved child care training or preparing material for use in connection with such training or (c)the cost of maintaining any person undergoing such training.

to.p: make grants to local authorities in respect of expenditure incurred by them in providing secure accommodation in community homes other than assisted community homes.
(with the consent of the Treasury) require the authority concerned to repay the grant, in whole or in part

ul.li: (a)a grant has been made under subsection (2) with respect to any secure accommodation but (b)the grant is not used for the purpose for which it was made or the accommodation is not used as,
or ceases to be used as,
secure accommodation.

to.p: make grants to voluntary organisations towards

ul.li: (a)expenditure incurred by them in connection with the establishment,
maintenance or improvement of voluntary homes which,

at the time when the expenditure was incurred (i)were assisted community homes or (ii)were designated as such or (b)expenses incurred in respect of the borrowing of money to defray any such expenditure.

to.p: arrange for the provision, equipment and maintenance of homes for the accommodation of children who are in need of particular facilities and services which

ul.li: (a)are or will be provided in those homes and (b)in the opinion of the Secretary of State,
are unlikely to be readily available in community homes.

to.p: conduct, or assist other persons in conducting, research into any matter connected with

ul.li: (a)his functions,
or the functions of local authorities,

under the enactments mentioned in subsection (9) (aa)the functions of (i)the Child Safeguarding Practice Review Panel (ii)safeguarding partners (within the meaning given by section 16E(3) of the Children Act 2004) in relation to local authority areas in England (iii)child death review partners (within the meaning given by section 16Q(2) of the Children Act 2004) in relation to local authority areas in England (b)the adoption of children or (c)the accommodation of children in a care home or independent hospital.

to.p: conduct, or assist other persons in conducting, research into any matter connected with—

ul.li: (a) their functions under the enactments mentioned in subsection (9);
(b) the adoption of children;
or (c) the accommodation of children in a care home or independent hospital.

to.p: at such times and in such form as the Secretary of State may direct, transmit to him such particulars as he may require with respect to—

ul.li: (a) the performance by the local authority of all or any of their functions— (i) under the enactments mentioned in subsection (9);
or (ii) in connection with the accommodation of children in a care home or independent hospital;
and (b) the children in relation to whom the authority have exercised those functions.

to.p: at such times and in such form as the Secretary of State may direct, transmit to him such particulars as he may require with respect to children accommodated by them or on their behalf.

for.p: particulars required to be transmitted under subsection (3) or (4)

to.p: include particulars relating to and identifying individual children.
direct an officer of the family court to transmit

ul.li: (a)to such person as may be specified in the direction and (b)at such times and in such form as he may direct,
such particulars as he may require with respect to proceedings of the court which relate to children.

to.p: in each year lay before Parliament a consolidated and classified abstract of the information transmitted to him under subsections (3) to (5).
institute research designed to provide information on which requests for information under this section may be based.
keep under review the adequacy of the provision of child care training and for that purpose shall receive and consider any information from or representations made by—

ul.li: (a) the Central Council for Education and Training in Social Work;
(b) such representatives of local authorities as appear to him to be appropriate;
or (c) such other persons or organisations as appear to him to be appropriate,
concerning the provision of such training.

to.p: make an order declaring any local authority to be in default with respect to that duty

condition.p: the Secretary of State is satisfied that the authority has failed, without reasonable excuse, to comply with any of the duties imposed on them by or under this Act.

to.p: notify the appropriate officer of the responsible authority

condition.p: a child is provided with accommodation in England by any Special Health Authority, National Health Service trust or NHS foundation trust or by a local authority in England in the exercise of education functions (“the accommodating authority”)—

ul.li: (a)for a consecutive period of at least three months or (b)with the intention,
on the part of that authority,
of accommodating him for such a period.

to.p: notify the appropriate officer of the responsible authority when they cease to accommodate the child

condition.p: subsection (1) applies with respect to a child.

to.p: apply in relation to the Secretary of State, NHS England or (as the case may be) the integrated care board as if it were the accommodating authority

condition.p: a child is provided with accommodation

ul.li: (a)by a body which is not mentioned in subsection (1),
and (b)pursuant to arrangements made by the Secretary of State,
NHS England or an integrated care board under the National Health Service Act 2006

(a)take such steps as are reasonably practicable to enable them to determine whether the child’s welfare is adequately safeguarded and promoted while he is accommodated by the accommodating authority and (b)consider the extent to which (if at all) they should exercise any of their functions under this Act with respect to the child

condition.p: the appropriate officer of a local authority in England has been notified under this section, or under section 120 of the Social Services and Well-being (Wales) Act 2014 (assessment of children accommodated by health authorities and education authorities).

to.p: treat the child as if he were in the area of the local authority, for the purposes of subsection (4)(b)

condition.p: the child is not in the area of the local authority.

for.p: the person carrying on the establishment in question

to.p: notify the appropriate officer of the local authority within whose area the establishment is carried on

condition.p: a child is provided with accommodation in England in any care home or independent hospital

ul.li: (a)for a consecutive period of at least three months or (b)with the intention,
on the part of the person taking the decision to accommodate him,
of accommodating him for such period.

for.p: the person carrying on the establishment

to.p: also notify the appropriate officer of that authority when he ceases to accommodate the child in the establishment

condition.p: subsection (1) applies with respect to a child.

ul.li: (a)take such steps as are reasonably practicable to enable them to determine whether the child’s welfare is adequately safeguarded and promoted while he is accommodated in the establishment in question and (b)consider the extent to which (if at all) they should exercise any of their functions under this Act with respect to the child

condition.p: the appropriate officer of a local authority has been notified under this section.

for.p: the person carrying on any care home or independent hospital

to.p: fail, without reasonable excuse, to comply with this section.

for.p: a person authorised by a local authority in England

to.p: enter any care home or independent hospital within the authority’s area for the purpose of establishing whether the requirements of this section have been complied with.
intentionally obstruct another in the exercise of the power of entry.

for.p: any person exercising the power of entry

to.p: produce some duly authenticated document showing his authority to do so

condition.p: so required.

to.p: in accordance with regulations made under this section, make arrangements for the child to be visited by a representative of the authority (“a representative”).
make provision about—

ul.li: (a) the frequency of visits under visiting arrangements;
(b) circumstances in which visiting arrangements must require a child to be visited;
and (c) additional functions of a representative.

to.p: satisfy themselves that the person chosen has the necessary skills and experience to perform the functions of a representative

condition.p: choosing a representative.

to.p: safeguard and promote the child’s welfare

condition.p: a school or college provides accommodation for any child.

to.p: take reasonably practicable steps to enable them to determine whether the child’s welfare is adequately safeguarded and promoted while accommodation for the child is provided by the school or college

condition.p: accommodation is provided for a child by any school or college the appropriate.

to.p: at any time (including a time when the duty under subsection (3) is suspended by virtue of section 87A) direct the Chief Inspector for England to take the steps referred to in subsection (3)

condition.p: accommodation is provided for a child by a school or college in England.

to.p: at any time when the duty under subsection (3) is suspended by virtue of section 87A, take the steps referred to in subsection (3)

condition.p: accommodation is provided for a child by a school or college in Wales.

ul.li: (a)in the case of a school other than an independent school,
an alternative provision Academy that is not an independent school or a special school,
notify the local authority for the area in which the school is situated (b)in the case of a special school which is maintained by a local authority,
notify that authority (c)in any other case,
notify the Secretary of State

condition.p: he is of the opinion that there has been a failure to comply with subsection (1) in relation to a child provided with accommodation by a school or college in England.

ul.li: (a)in the case of a school other than an independent school or a special school,
notify the local authority for the area in which the school is situated (b)in the case of a special school which is maintained by a local authority,
notify that authority

condition.p: they are of the opinion that there has been a failure to comply with subsection (1) in relation to a child provided with accommodation by a school or college in Wales.

for.p: a person authorised by the appropriate authority

to.p: for the purpose of enabling that authority to discharge any of its functions under this section, enter at any time premises which are, or are to be, premises of the school or college

condition.p: accommodation is, or is to be, provided for a child by any school or college.

for.p: any person exercising the power conferred by subsection (5)

to.p: carry out such inspection of premises, children and records as is prescribed by regulations made by the Secretary of State for the purposes of this section.
produce some duly authenticated document showing his authority to do so

condition.p: asked to do so.

for.p: any person authorised by the regulations to inspect records

to.p: be entitled at any reasonable time to have access to, and inspect and check the operation of, any computer and any associated apparatus or material which is or has been in use in connection with the records in question.

for.p: any person authorised by the regulations to inspect records

to.p: require— (i) the person by whom or on whose behalf the computer is or has been so used; or (ii) any person having charge of, or otherwise concerned with the operation of, the computer, apparatus or material, to afford him such assistance as he may reasonably require.
intentionally obstruct another in the exercise of any power conferred by this section or the regulations.
publish a report on whether the child’s welfare is adequately safeguarded and promoted while accommodation for the child is provided by the school or college

condition.p: the Chief Inspector for England or the National Assembly for Wales exercises the power conferred by subsection (5) in relation to a child.

for.p: Chief Inspector for England or the National Assembly for Wales

ul.li: (a)send a copy of the report to the school or college concerned and (b)make copies of the report available for inspection at its offices by any person at any reasonable time

condition.p: it publishes a report under this section.

to.p: appoint a person to be an inspector for the purposes of this section

ul.li: (a)that person already acts as an inspector for other purposes in relation to schools or colleges to which section 87(1) applies,

and (b)the Secretary of State is satisfied that the person is an appropriate person to determine whether the welfare of children provided with accommodation by such schools or colleges is adequately safeguarded and promoted while accommodation for the children is provided by them.

for.p: the appropriate authority’s duty under section 87(3) in relation to any school or college that has been suspended

to.p: cease to be suspended under this section

condition.p: the appropriate authority receive

ul.li: (a)a notice under subsection (4) relating to the inspector,
or (b)a notice under subsection (5) relating to the relevant agreement.
(a) the relevant person enters into an agreement in writing with a person appointed under subsection (1),
(b) the agreement provides for the person so appointed to have in relation to the school or college the function of determining whether section 87(1) is being complied with,
and (c) the appropriate authority receive from the person mentioned in paragraph (b) (“the inspector”) notice in writing that the agreement has come into effect

to.p: terminate a person’s appointment under subsection (1)

ul.li: (a) that person so requests,
or (b) the Secretary of State ceases,
in relation to that person,
to be satisfied that he is such a person as is mentioned in paragraph (b) of that subsection.

to.p: give notice of the termination of that person’s appointment to the appropriate authority.

ul.li: (a) that person so requests,
or (b) the Secretary of State ceases,
in relation to that person,
to be satisfied that he is such a person as is mentioned in paragraph (b) of that subsection.

to.p: by regulations specify matters that must be taken into account

condition.p: deciding whether to appoint a person to be an inspector for the purposes of this section in relation to schools or colleges in England, or to terminate the appointment of such a person under subsection (4)(b).

to.p: give to the appropriate authority notice in writing of the fact that it has ceased to have effect.

ul.li: (a)the appropriate authority’s duty under section 87(3) in relation to any school or college is suspended under this section,
and (b)the relevant agreement ceases to have effect.

to.p: impose on a person appointed under section 87A(1) (“an authorised inspector”) such requirements relating to, or in connection with, the carrying out under substitution agreements of the function mentioned in section 87A(2)(b) as the Secretary of State thinks fit.
give notice of that fact

ul.li: (a)in the case of a school other than an independent school ,
an alternative provision Academy that is not an independent school or a special school,
to the local authority for the area in which the school is situated (b)in the case of a special school which is maintained by a local authority,
to that authority (c)in any other case,
to the Secretary of State

condition.p: , in the course of carrying out under a substitution agreement the function mentioned in section 87A(2)(b), it appears to the authorised inspector that there has been a failure to comply with section 87(1) in the case of a child provided with accommodation by the school or college to which the agreement relates.

ul.li: (a)give notice of that fact to the local authority in whose area the school or college is situated,
and (b)where the inspector is required to make inspection reports to the Secretary of State,
supply that local authority with a copy of the latest inspection report to have been made by the inspector to the Secretary of State in relation to the school or college

condition.p: , in the course of carrying out under a substitution agreement the function mentioned in section 87A(2)(b), it appears to the authorised inspector that a child provided with accommodation by the school or college to which the agreement relates is suffering, or is likely to suffer, significant harm.

to.p: at intervals of no more than a year, prepare and send to the Secretary of State a report about inspectors who are appointed under section 87A in relation to schools or colleges in England.
have regard to such matters as the Secretary of State may direct

condition.p: preparing a report under this section.

to.p: in particular give directions about—

ul.li: (a) matters to be taken into account in preparing a report,
and (b) the form and contents of a report.

to.p: prepare and publish statements of national minimum standards for safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children for whom accommodation is provided by a school or college.
keep the standards set out in the statements under review and may publish amended statements whenever he considers it appropriate to do so.
consult any persons he considers appropriate

condition.p: issuing a statement, or an amended statement which in the opinion of the Secretary of State effects a substantial change in the standards.

to.p: be made in relation to any school or college in respect of which the appropriate authority is required to take steps under section 87(3).
by regulations require the relevant person to pay the appropriate authority an annual fee of such amount, and within such time, as the regulations may specify.

for.p: the powers given by that Act to include requirements in supervision orders

to.p: have effect

condition.p: amendments made by Schedule 12.

to.p: have effect

condition.p: the emergency protection order
an emergency protection order is made with respect to a child who is in care

for.p: any order made under section 4(1) 4ZA(1),4A(1) or 5(1)

to.p: continue in force until the child reaches the age of eighteen

condition.p: it is brought to an end earlier.

ul.li: (a)agreement under section 4 ,
4ZA or 4A or (b)appointment under section 5(3) or (4)

to.p: continue in force until the child reaches the age of eighteen

condition.p: it is brought to an end earlier.

to.p: cease to have effect when the child reaches the age of sixteen

condition.p: it would otherwise still be in force
it is to have effect beyond that age by virtue of section 9(6).

to.p: cease to have effect when he reaches the age of eighteen

condition.p: it would otherwise still be in force
a section 8 order has effect with respect to a child who has reached the age of sixteen

for.p: any care order, other than an interim care order

to.p: continue in force until the child reaches the age of eighteen

condition.p: it is brought to an end earlier.

for.p: any order made under any other provision of this Act in relation to a child

to.p: cease to have effect when he reaches the age of eighteen

condition.p: it would otherwise still be in force.

to.p: (whether or not it makes any other order in response to the application) order that no application for an order under this Act of any specified kind may be made with respect to the child concerned by any person named in the order without leave of the court

condition.p: disposing of any application for an order under this Act.

for.p: a further application of a kind mentioned in paragraphs

ul.li: (a) to (e)

to.p: be made with respect to the child concerned, without leave of the court

condition.p: an application (“the previous application”) has been made for—

ul.li: (a) the discharge of a care order;
(b) the discharge of a supervision order;
(c) the discharge of an education supervision order;
(d) the substitution of a supervision order for a care order;
or (e) a child assessment order,

condition.p: the period between the disposal of the previous application and the making of the further application exceeds six months.

to.p: make a further application for such an order with respect to the same child

ul.li: (a) the person has made an application for an order under section 34,
(b) the application has been refused,
and (c) a period of less than six months has elapsed since the refusal

condition.p: he has obtained the leave of the court.

to.p: consider whether there has been a material change of circumstances since the order was made

condition.p: a person who is named in a section 91(14) order applies for leave to make an application of a specified kind
determining whether to grant leave.

to.p: make a section 91(14) order

ul.li: (a)on an application made (i)by the relevant individual,
(ii)by or on behalf of the child concerned,
(iii)by any other person who is a party to the application being disposed of by the court,
(b)of its own motion.

for.p: an authority having power to make rules of court

to.p: make such provision for giving effect to

ul.li: (a)this Act (b)the provisions of any statutory instrument made under this Act,
or (c)any amendment made by this Act in any other enactment,
as appears to that authority to be necessary or expedient.

to.p: make provision

ul.li: (a)with respect to the procedure to be followed in any relevant proceedings (including the manner in which any application is to be made or other proceedings commenced),
(b)as to the persons entitled to participate in any relevant proceedings,
whether as parties to the proceedings or by being given the opportunity to make representations to the court,
(bb)for children to be separately represented in relevant proceedings (c)with respect to the documents and information to be furnished,
and notices to be given,
in connection with any relevant proceedings,
(e)with respect to preliminary hearings,
(h)enabling the court,
in such circumstances as may be prescribed,
to proceed on any application even though the respondent has not been given notice of the proceedings.

to.p: order the child concerned to attend such stage or stages of the proceedings as may be specified in the order

condition.p: dealing with any proceedings in which a court is hearing an application for an order under Part IV or V, or is considering whether to make any such order.

to.p: be exercised in accordance with rules of court
make an order authorising a constable, or such person as may be specified in the order,

ul.li: (a)to take charge of the child and to bring him to the court,
and (b)to enter and search any premises specified in the order if he has reasonable cause to believe that the child may be found on the premises.

to.p: order any person who is in a position to do so to bring the child to the court.
order a person has information about the whereabouts of the child to disclose it to the court

condition.p: the court has reason to believe that the person has information about the whereabouts of the child.

to.p: be heard by the court

condition.p: in its opinion

ul.li: (a)he understands that it is his duty to speak the truth,
and (b)he has sufficient understanding to justify his evidence being heard.

for.p: The Lord of Justice Chancellor, with occurence of the Lord Chief Justice

to.p: make provision, by order, for the admissibility of evidence which would otherwise be inadmissible under any rule of law relating to hearsay.
only be made with respect to

ul.li: (a)civil proceedings in general or such civil proceedings,
or class of civil proceedings,
as may be prescribed,
and (b)evidence in connection with the upbringing,
maintenance or welfare of a child.
(a)in particular,
provide for the admissibility of statements which are made orally or in a prescribed form or which are recorded by any prescribed method of recording,

(b)make different provision for different purposes and in relation to different descriptions of court and (c)may make such amendments and repeals in any enactment relating to evidence (other than in this Act) as the Lord Chancellor considers necessary or expedient in consequence of the provision made by the order.

to.p: publish to the public at large or any section of the public any material which is intended, or likely, to identify,

ul.li: (a)any child as being involved in any proceedings before the High Court or the family court in which any power under this Act or the Adoption and Children Act 2002 may be exercised by the court with respect to that or any other child,
or (b)an address or school as being that of a child involved in any such proceedings.

for.p: the court or the Lord Chancellor

to.p: dispense with the requirements of subsection (2), by order, to such extent as may be specified in the order.

condition.p: satisfied that the welfare of the child requires it and, in the case of the Lord Chancellor
the Lord Chief Justice agrees

to.p: nominate a judicial office holder (as defined in section 109(4) of the Constitutional Reform Act 2005) to exercise his functions under subsection (4).
give evidence on any matter, and to answer any question put to him in the course of giving evidence

condition.p: in proceedings in which a court is hearing an application for an order under Part IV or V, EVEN IF doing so might incriminate him or his spouse or civil partner of an offence.

for.p: a statement or admission made in such proceedings

to.p: be admissible in evidence against the person making it or his spouse or civil partner in proceedings for an offence other than perjury.
exercise the High Court’s inherent jurisdiction with respect to children,

ul.li: (a)so as to require a child to be placed in the care,
or put under the supervision,
of a local authority,
(b)so as to require a child to be accommodated by or on behalf of a local authority,
(c)so as to make a child who is the subject of a care order a ward of court,
or (d)for the purpose of conferring on any local authority power to determine any question which has arisen,
or which may arise,
in connection with any aspect of parental responsibility for a child.

for.p: any application for any exercise of the court’s inherent jurisdiction with respect to children

to.p: be made by a local authority

condition.p: the authority have obtained the leave of the court.

to.p: grant leave

condition.p: it is satisfied that,

ul.li: (a)the result which the authority wish to achieve could not be achieved through the making of any order of a kind to which subsection (5) applies,
and (b)there is reasonable cause to believe that if the court’s inherent jurisdiction is not exercised with respect to the child he is likely to suffer significant harm.

to.p: make regulations providing

ul.li: (a)for prescribed orders which (i)are made by a court in Northern Ireland,
and (ii)appear to the Secretary of State to correspond in their effect to orders which may be made under any provision of this Act,
to have effect in prescribed circumstances,
for prescribed purposes of this Act,
as if they were orders of a prescribed kind made under this Act,
(b)for prescribed orders which (i)are made by a court in England and Wales,
and (ii)appear to the Secretary of State to correspond in their effect to orders which may be made under any provision in force in Northern Ireland,
to have effect in prescribed circumstances,
for prescribed purposes of the law of Northern Ireland,
as if they were orders of a prescribed kind made in Northern Ireland.

to.p: provide for the order concerned to cease to have effect for the purposes of the law of Northern Ireland, or (as the case may be) the law of England and Wales

condition.p: prescribed conditions are satisfied.

to.p: make regulations providing for prescribed orders which,

ul.li: (a)are made by a court in the Isle of Man or in any of the Channel Islands,
and (b)appear to the Secretary of State to correspond in their effect to orders which may be made under this Act,
to have effect in prescribed circumstances for prescribed purposes of this Act,
as if they were orders of a prescribed kind made under this Act.

for.p: any regulation made under this section

ul.li: (a)make such consequential amendments (including repeals) in,

(i)section 25 of the Children and Young Persons Act 1969 (transfers between England and Wales and Northern Ireland) or (ii)section 26 (transfers between England and Wales and Channel Islands or Isle of Man) of that Act,
as the Secretary of State considers necessary or expedient,
and (b)modify any provision of this Act,
in its application (by virtue of the regulations) in relation to an order made otherwise than in England and Wales.

to.p: issue a warrant authorising any constable to assist that person in the exercise of those powers, using reasonable force if necessary

condition.p: on an application made by any person for a warrant under this section, it appears to the court,

ul.li: (a)that a person attempting to exercise powers under any enactment mentioned in subsection (6) has been prevented from doing so by being refused entry to the premises concerned or refused access to the child concerned or (b)that any such person is likely to be so prevented from exercising any such powers.

for.p: every warrant issued under this section

to.p: be addressed to, and executed by, a constable who shall be accompanied by the person applying for the warrant

ul.li: (a)that person so desires,
and (b)the court by whom the warrant is issued does not direct otherwise.

for.p: a court granting an application for a warrant under this section

to.p: direct that the constable concerned may, in executing the warrant, be accompanied by a registered medical practitioner, registered nurse or registered midwife if he so chooses.

for.p: an application for a warrant under this section

to.p: be made in the manner and form prescribed by the rules of court.

for.p: the application and any warrant granted on the application

to.p: name the child

ul.li: (a)an application for a warrant under this section relates to a particular child,
and (b)it is reasonably practicable to do so,

for.p: the application and any warrant granted on the application

to.p: describe the child as clearly as possible

condition.p: it does not name him

ul.li: (a)an application for a warrant under this section relates to a particular child,
and (b)it is reasonably practicable to do so,

for.p: any power of the Lord Chancellor , the Treasury , the Secretary of State or the National Assembly for Wales under this Act to make an order, regulations, or rules, except an order under section 54(2), 56(4)

ul.li: 84 or 97(4) or paragraph 1(1) of Schedule 4

to.p: be exercisable by statutory instrument.
be made

condition.p: a draft of it has been laid before, and approved by a resolution of, each House of Parliament

for.p: an order under section 4(1B) , 4ZA(3) or 17(4) or regulations which fall within subsection (3AA), (3AB), (3B) , (3BA) , (3BB) or (3C)

to.p: be made by the Secretary of State

condition.p: a draft of the statutory instrument containing the order or regulations has been laid before, and approved by a resolution of, each House of Parliament.

for.p: any statutory instrument made under this Act

ul.li: (a)make different provision for different cases (b)provide for exemptions from any of its provisions;
and (c)contain incidental,
supplemental and transitional provisions.

for.p: any power of the Welsh Ministers under section 31A or Part 7 to make regulations

to.p: be exercisable by statutory instrument.

for.p: any notice or other document required under this Act to be served on any person

to.p: be served on any person may be served on him by being delivered personally to him, or being sent by post to him in a registered letter or by the recorded delivery service at his proper address.

for.p: any notice or other document required to be served on a body corporate or a firm

to.p: be served on the secretary or clerk of that body, or on a partner of that firm.

ul.li: (a)grants made by the Secretary of State under this Act and (b)any other expenses incurred by the Secretary of State under this Act,

to.p: be payable out of money provided by Parliament.

for.p: any sums received by the Secretary of State under section 58, or by way of the repayment of any grant made under section 82(2) or (4)

to.p: be paid into the Consolidated Fund.
by Order in Council direct that any of the provisions of this Act shall extend to any of the Channel Islands with such exceptions and modifications as may be specified in the Order.
be cited as the Children Act 1989.
be appointed for different provisions of this Act and in relation to different cases.

for.p: the minor amendments set out in Schedule 12

to.p: have effect

for.p: the consequential amendments set out in Schedule 13

to.p: have effect

for.p: transitional provisions and savings set out in Schedule 14

to.p: have effect.

for.p: the repeals set out in Schedule 15

to.p: have effect
make such transitional provisions or savings as appear necessary or expedient in connection with the provisions brought into force by the order, including—

ul.li: (a) provisions adding to or modifying the provisions of Schedule 14,
and (b) such adaptations— (i) of the provisions brought into force by the order;
and (ii) of any provisions of this Act then in force,

condition.p: as appear necessary or expedient in consequence of the partial operation of this Act.

to.p: make such amendments or repeals in the enactments specified in the order

condition.p: they appear necessary or expedient in consequence of any provision of this Act.
